message.content:
你是引用核验员。用 ToolSearch 加载 WebFetch(必要时 WebSearch),逐条实访下列 URL,判断:URL 是否可达、页面内容是否真的支持对应声称(标题/主题对得上即可,不苛求逐字)。对打不开或内容对不上的,记入 dead_or_fabricated。输出中文。

角度: usability-review
- 10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design — NN/G: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/
  声称: Nielsen 10 条可用性启发式(可见系统状态/贴合真实世界/用户控制与自由/一致性与标准/防错/识别优于记忆/灵活高效/美观极简/帮助用户识别诊断并从错误恢复/帮助与文档)自1994年起30年未变,官方明言同样适用于桌面(desktop)、移动、Web,可作 SaaS/仪表盘/应用的通用评审骨架。
- Severity Ratings for Usability Problems — NN/G: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/how-to-rate-the-severity-of-usability-problems/
  声称: 启发式评审用 0-4 级严重度打分:0=非可用性问题,1=仅外观问题(有余力再改),2=次要(低优先级),3=主要(高优先级必改),4=灾难(发布前必须修);严重度由 frequency(频率)、impact(克服难度)、persistence(是否反复)三因素综合,外加 market impact 考量。
- Severity Ratings for Usability Problems — NN/G: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/how-to-rate-the-severity-of-usability-problems/
  声称: Nielsen 建议用「3 名评审员评分的均值」作为严重度评级的实用标准:单人评级不可靠,增加评审员质量快速提升。发现率经验值:1 人约 35%,3 人约 75%,5 人约 85%,3-5 人可覆盖 74%-87% 的可用性问题,超过 5 人边际收益递减。
- macOS HIG — User Interaction / Drag and Drop: https://developers.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/user-interaction/drag-and-drop/
  声称: macOS 是指针驱动平台:每个可交互元素都必须响应 hover/click/right-click/drag,且所有可交互元素必须有可见的 hover 悬停态——这是移动端没有、桌面评审必须专门验收的维度。

attachment.content:
- archive-research-pack: Use when 一次多路 workflow 调研 / deep-research / pre-mortem oracle 搜证收口,需要把成果落盘防失忆。症状:调研完只想存一个总报告、transcript 在 /tmp 待清会丢、finder 引的 source URL 事后无法复核、各路一手档与综合报告混在一起。
- axiom-accessibility: Use when fixing or auditing ANY accessibility issue — VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, color contrast, touch targets, WCAG compliance, App Store accessibility review.
- axiom-ai: Use when implementing ANY Apple Intelligence or on-device AI feature. Covers Foundation Models, @Generable, LanguageModelSession, structured output, Tool protocol, iOS 26 AI integration.
- axiom-apple-docs: Use when ANY question involves Apple framework APIs, Swift compiler errors, or Xcode-bundled documentation. Covers Liquid Glass, Swift 6.2 concurrency, Foundation Models, SwiftData, StoreKit, 32 Swift compiler diagnostics.
- axiom-build: Use when ANY iOS build fails, test crashes, Xcode misbehaves, or environment issue occurs before debugging code. Covers build failures, compilation errors, dependency conflicts, simulator problems, environment-first diagnostics.
- axiom-concurrency: Use when writing ANY async code, actors, threads, or seeing ANY concurrency error. Covers Swift 6 concurrency, @MainActor, Sendable, data races, async/await patterns.
- axiom-data: Use when working with ANY data persistence, database, storage, CloudKit, migration, or serialization. Covers SwiftData, Core Data, GRDB, SQLite, CloudKit sync, file storage, Codable, migrations.
- axiom-design: Use when making design decisions, implementing HIG patterns, Liquid Glass, SF Symbols, typography, or structuring app entry points and authentication flows.
- axiom-games: Use when building ANY 2D or 3D game with SpriteKit, SceneKit, or RealityKit, or adding touch controls or game controller support. Covers scene graphs, ECS, physics, actions, game loops, rendering, TouchController, GCController.
- axiom-graphics: Use when working with ANY GPU rendering, Metal, OpenGL migration, shaders, 3D content, RealityKit, AR, USD/USDZ files, or display performance. Covers Metal migration, shader conversion, RealityKit ECS, RealityView, USDKit, ProMotion.
- axiom-health: Use when working with HealthKit, WorkoutKit, health data, workouts, or fitness features on iOS or watchOS. Covers permissions, queries, background delivery, custom workouts, multidevice coordination.
- axiom-integration: Use when integrating ANY iOS system feature - Siri, Shortcuts, widgets, IAP, localization, privacy, alarms, calendar, reminders, contacts, background tasks, push notifications, timers. Covers App Intents, WidgetKit, StoreKit, EventKit, Contacts.
- axiom-location: Use when implementing location services, maps, geofencing, or debugging location/MapKit issues. Covers Core Location, CLMonitor, MapKit, annotations, directions.
- axiom-macos: Use when building ANY macOS app — windows, menus, sandboxing, distribution, AppKit bridging or modernization (control events, state restoration, concentric corners), or macOS-specific SwiftUI patterns.
- axiom-media: Use when working with camera, photos, audio, haptics, ShazamKit, or Now Playing. Covers AVCaptureSession, PHPicker, PhotosPicker, AVFoundation, Core Haptics, audio recognition, MediaPlayer, CarPlay, MusicKit.
- axiom-networking: Use when implementing or debugging ANY network connection, API call, or socket. Covers URLSession, Network.framework, NetworkConnection, connection diagnostics.
- axiom-payments: Use when accepting ANY real-world payment — Apple Pay, Wallet passes, Tap to Pay, Orders in Wallet. NOT in-app purchase / digital content (use axiom-integration). Covers entitlements, certs, signing, App Review payment rules.
- axiom-performance: Use when app feels slow, memory grows, battery drains, or diagnosing ANY performance issue. Covers memory leaks, profiling, Instruments workflows, retain cycles, performance optimization.
- axiom-security: Use when storing credentials securely, encrypting data, implementing passkeys, securing AI/agentic features against prompt injection, code signing, or managing certificates and provisioning profiles.
- axiom-shipping: Use when preparing ANY app for submission, handling App Store rejections, writing appeals, or managing App Store Connect. Covers submission checklists, rejection troubleshooting, metadata requirements, privacy manifests, age ratings, export compliance.
- axiom-swift: Use when reviewing Swift code for modern idioms, working with noncopyable types, implementing drag and drop, adding debug deep links, or building for tvOS.
- axiom-swiftui: Use when building, fixing, or improving ANY SwiftUI UI — views, navigation, layout, animations, performance, architecture, gestures, debugging, iOS 26 features.
- axiom-testing: Use when writing ANY test, debugging flaky tests, making tests faster, or choosing Swift Testing vs XCTest. Covers unit tests, UI tests, async testing, test architecture.
- axiom-tools: Use when asking how to use Axiom or what skills exist, capturing console with xclog, symbolicating .ips/MetricKit/.crash crashes with xcsym, driving/validating simulator UI & accessibility with xcui, or analyzing xctrace/CPU profiles with xcprof.
- axiom-uikit: Use when bridging UIKit and SwiftUI, modernizing UIKit apps (scene lifecycle, resizability), debugging Auto Layout, Combine, TextKit, PencilKit, or UIKit animations.
- axiom-vision: Use when implementing ANY computer vision feature — image analysis, pose detection, person segmentation, subject lifting, text recognition, barcode scanning.
- axiom-watchos: Use when building ANY watchOS app — app structure, independent apps, Watch Connectivity, Smart Stack widgets, complications, controls, RelevanceKit, background tasks, ClockKit migration.
- axiom-xcode-mcp: Use when connecting to Xcode via MCP, using xcrun mcpbridge, or working with ANY Xcode MCP tool (XcodeRead, BuildProject, RunTests, RenderPreview). Covers setup, tool reference, workflow patterns, troubleshooting.
- closeout-receipt-writer: Use when 一个 apply/重型任务/审计 wave 收口需要写 receipt 证据。症状:想用 hardcoded pass、读 metadata flag 自证 verdict、凭印象写状态、或 receipt 与实跑 log 脱节;0-34 灾难根因正是 receipt 读自己的 metadata flag 翻 pass、零 token 证据。
- doc-cascade-sweep: Use when 范式翻案/架构大改/口径回写后,grep 全仓命中几十个文件含过期锚点或旧数字,需要全仓级联更新。症状:看到 grep 命中 60+ 文件就想 sed 全量替换、或一个决策只 append 进一个文件没回写文档组、或改了带日期的历史快照正文破坏一手溯源。
- ios-debugger-agent: Build, run, and debug iOS apps on Simulator with XcodeBuildMCP. Use when launching an app, inspecting simulator UI or logs, or diagnosing runtime behavior.
- ios-ettrace-performance: Capture and interpret iOS Simulator ETTrace profiles. Use when profiling launch or runtime latency, comparing traces, or finding CPU-heavy stacks.
- ios-simulator-skill: 29 production-ready scripts for iOS app testing, building, and automation. Provides semantic UI navigation, build automation, accessibility testing, and simulator lifecycle management. Optimized for AI agents with minimal token output.
- openspec-apply-change: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Use when the user wants to start implementing, continue implementation, or work through tasks.
- openspec-archive-change: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow. Use when the user wants to finalize and archive a change after implementation is complete.
- openspec-explore: Enter explore mode - a thinking partner for exploring ideas, investigating problems, and clarifying requirements. Use when the user wants to think through something before or during a change.
- openspec-propose: Propose a new change with all artifacts generated in one step. Use when the user wants to quickly describe what they want to build and get a complete proposal with design, specs, and tasks ready for implementation.
- openspec-sync-specs: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs. Use when the user wants to update main specs with changes from a delta spec, without archiving the change.
- verify-external-claims: Use when 一个多路 workflow/finder 调研、deep-research、或综合官报告引用了 arxiv ID、精确数字（调用数/百分比/star/benchmark）、或 repo 活跃度断言,且这些要驱动决策。症状:finder 在搜证压力下高发编造精确数字 + 引用 ID,且常安到真实存在的 source 上(issue/论文真、数字假),综合官即使做了本机 grep 也漏外部声称。
- opsx:apply: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change (Experimental)
- opsx:archive: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow
- opsx:explore: Enter explore mode - think through ideas, investigate problems, clarify requirements
- opsx:propose: Propose a new change - create it and generate all artifacts in one step
- opsx:sync: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll load the fetch tools and verify each URL.
I'll fetch the three unique URLs (the severity one is checked twice for different claims).

input.prompt: List the 10 usability heuristics named on this page. Does it state they date from 1994 and have remained unchanged? Does it say they apply to desktop, mobile, and web interfaces?

Describe the 0-4 severity rating scale (what does each level 0,1,2,3,4 mean?). What three factors determine severity (frequency, impact, persistence) and is market impact mentioned? Does it recommend using the mean of ratings from about 3 evaluators? What does it say about number of evaluators and percentage of usability problems found (e.g. 1 evaluator ~35%, 3 evaluators ~75%, 5 evaluators)?
Does this page exist and load? What is it about (drag and drop on macOS)? Does it discuss hover states, pointer interactions, click, right-click for interactive elements?

content.content:
# Jakob Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics

The 10 heuristics listed are:

1. Visibility of System Status
2. Match Between the System and the Real World
3. User Control and Freedom
4. Consistency and Standards
5. Error Prevention
6. Recognition Rather than Recall
7. Flexibility and Efficiency of Use
8. Aesthetic and Minimalist Design
9. Help Users Recognize, Diagnose, and Recover from Errors
10. Help and Documentation

## Timeline Confirmation

Yes, the article confirms the 1994 origin and ongoing relevance: "the **10 heuristics themselves have remained relevant and unchanged since 1994.** When something has remained true for 26 years, it will likely apply to future generations of user interfaces as well."

## Platform Scope

The article does **not** state that these heuristics apply specifically to desktop, mobile, and web interfaces. However, it provides broader application examples, including "10 Usability Heuristics Applied to Complex Applications," "Virtual Reality," and "Video Games," suggesting the framework's universal applicability across different interface types.

REDIRECT DETECTED: The URL redirects to a different host.

Original URL: https://developers.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/user-interaction/drag-and-drop/
Redirect URL: https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/user-interaction/drag-and-drop/
Status: 302 Found

To complete your request, I need to fetch content from the redirected URL. Please use WebFetch again with these parameters:
- url: "https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/user-interaction/drag-and-drop/"
- prompt: "Does this page exist and load? What is it about (drag and drop on macOS)? Does it discuss hover states, pointer interactions, click, right-click for interactive elements?"

# Severity Rating Scale Summary

## The 0-4 Scale

The article defines each level as follows:

- **0**: "I don't agree that this is a usability problem at all"
- **1**: "Cosmetic problem only: need not be fixed unless extra time is available"
- **2**: "Minor usability problem: fixing this should be given low priority"
- **3**: "Major usability problem: important to fix, so should be given high priority"
- **4**: "Usability catastrophe: imperative to fix this before product can be released"

## Severity Determinants

The piece identifies three core factors influencing severity:

1. **Frequency**: How often the problem occurs
2. **Impact**: How difficult users find it to overcome the issue
3. **Persistence**: Whether it's a one-time obstacle or recurring annoyance

Yes, market impact is mentioned as an additional consideration: "certain usability problems can have a devastating effect on the popularity of a product, even if they are 'objectively' quite easy to overcome."

## Evaluator Recommendations

The article strongly recommends using "the mean of a set of ratings from three evaluators" as satisfactory for practical purposes. It emphasizes that single evaluator ratings are "too unreliable to be trusted," while noting that quality improves significantly as more evaluators participate.

However, the document does not provide specific percentages of problems discovered by different numbers of evaluators.

content.text: The Apple URL redirects (developers→developer). Let me follow it.

input.prompt: Does this page exist and load with real content? What is it about? Does it discuss hover states, pointer interactions, click, right-click, drag for interactive elements on macOS? Or does it redirect / show a generic HIG landing page?

content.content:
I don't have enough information to answer your question definitively. The content you've provided only shows the page title "Drag and drop | Apple Developer Documentation" — there's no actual page content included.

To properly assess whether this page:
- Exists and loads with real content
- Discusses hover states, pointer interactions, click, right-click, and drag behaviors for macOS

I would need you to provide the actual body content of the page. Could you share:

1. The full HTML/text content of the page, or
2. A description of what appears when you visit it, or
3. The actual URL so I can help you understand what to expect?

If you can paste the page content, I'll be happy to analyze whether it covers the interactive elements and macOS-specific interactions you're asking about.

content.text: The Apple page returns only a title (a JS-driven stub with no substantive body). Let me search for the actual current content and whether it supports the specific claim.

input.query: Apple Human Interface Guidelines macOS drag and drop pointer hover state interactive elements

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Apple Human Interface Guidelines macOS drag and drop pointer hover state interactive elements"

Links: [{"title":"Drag and drop | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/drag-and-drop"},{"title":"Human Interface Guidelines | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/macos/human-interface-guidelines/user-interaction/drag-and-drop/"},{"title":"Accessibility | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/accessibility/overview/user-interaction/"},{"title":"Menus and actions | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/menus-and-actions"},{"title":"Pointing devices | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/ios/user-interaction/pointers/"},{"title":"Supporting drag and drop | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/tutorials/app-dev-training/supporting-drag-and-drop"},{"title":"Components | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/components/all-components"},{"title":"Adopting drag and drop in a custom view | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/drag_and_drop/adopting_drag_and_drop_in_a_custom_view"},{"title":"Pull-down buttons | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/pull-down-buttons"},{"title":"Pointer interactions | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/pointer-interactions"}]

I've performed the web search for "Apple Human Interface Guidelines macOS drag drop pointer hover state interactive elements." The search returned several relevant results from Apple's developer documentation:

**Key Results:**

1. **Drag and Drop Documentation** - Using drag and drop, people can move or duplicate selected photos, text, and other content by dragging the selection from one location to another.

2. **macOS-Specific Guidance** - On macOS, users can drag an item from one place in an app to another location. With these drag-and-drop operations, users can perform actions like moving or copying an item.

3. **Pointing Devices** - People can use a pointing device like a trackpad or mouse to navigate the interface and initiate actions.

The search results point to official Apple Developer Documentation resources, including:
- The main Drag and Drop Human Interface Guidelines page
- The macOS-specific Drag and Drop guidelines
- Pointer interactions documentation
- Accessibility guidelines for user interaction

For more detailed information about specific pointer hover states and interactive element behavior, you can visit the direct Apple Developer Documentation links provided in the search results, particularly the macOS Human Interface Guidelines section on drag and drop.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: What does this page cover? Does it state that every interactive element on macOS must respond to hover, click, right-click and drag, and that all interactive elements must have a visible hover state? Or is it only about drag-and-drop operations (moving/copying content)?

content.content:
# Response

I cannot provide a meaningful answer based on the content provided. The excerpt you shared only contains a page title ("Drag and drop | Apple Developer Documentation") with no actual page content.

To answer your question accurately, I would need:

1. **The full page content** from the Apple Developer Documentation on drag and drop
2. **The actual sections and guidelines** discussing interactive elements

## What I can tell you:

Based on your question's specificity, you're asking whether the page covers:
- **General interactive element requirements** (hover states, click/right-click/drag responses across macOS)
- OR
- **Drag-and-drop specific guidance** (content movement/copying operations)

Without the actual content, I cannot confirm which it addresses. 

**To get this answer, I recommend:**
- Visit the full Apple Developer Documentation page on Drag and Drop
- Check the macOS Human Interface Guidelines for general interactive element standards
- Look for separate sections on "Accessibility," "Hit targets," or "Interactive elements" if you need broad interaction requirements

If you can provide the actual page content, I'll give you a precise answer to your question.

content.text:
I have enough to assess all four. Summary of findings:

**Verified:**
- NN/G Ten Heuristics: URL live, all 10 heuristic names correct, "unchanged since 1994" confirmed verbatim. The "applies to desktop/mobile/web" phrasing is a reasonable paraphrase of the article's universal-applicability stance (it shows applications to complex apps, VR, games and says it "will likely apply to future generations of UIs"), though it doesn't literally enumerate "desktop, mobile, web."
- NN/G Severity, claim #1 (0-4 scale, 3 factors, market impact): fully supported, wording matches.

**Issues:**
- NN/G Severity, claim #2: the "mean of 3 evaluators / single rating unreliable" part IS on this page. But the discovery-rate percentages (1 person ~35%, 3 ~75%, 5 ~85%, 3-5 covers 74-87%) are NOT on this severity page — they belong to Nielsen's separate heuristic-evaluation / number-of-evaluators articles. Misattributed to this URL.
- Apple HIG drag-and-drop: the cited URL uses the legacy `developers.apple.com/.../macos/user-interaction/` path that 302-redirects (canonical is `/design/human-interface-guidelines/drag-and-drop`). More importantly, this is a drag-and-drop page; it does not support the sweeping claim that "every interactive element must respond to hover/click/right-click/drag and have a visible hover state." That hover/pointer content lives in the "Pointing devices"/"Pointer interactions" pages, not drag-and-drop.

checked.note: URL 可达。10 条启发式名称全部准确;页面原文确认「the 10 heuristics themselves have remained relevant and unchanged since 1994」。声称的「官方明言同样适用于桌面/移动/Web」略有拔高:文章未逐字列出 desktop/mobile/web,但明确主张跨界通用(附有复杂应用/VR/视频游戏的应用示例,并称「likely apply to future generations of UIs」),精神一致,可作通用评审骨架。
第一条声称完全成立:0=非可用性问题、1=仅外观(有余力再改)、2=次要低优先、3=主要高优先必改、4=灾难发布前必修,措辞与原文吻合;严重度由 frequency/impact/persistence 三因素综合,并另提 market impact(某些问题即使客观易克服也会重创产品口碑)。

第二条部分成立部分错源:「用 3 名评审员评分的均值、单人评级不可靠」确在本页(原文 mean of three evaluators / single rating too unreliable)。但发现率经验值(1 人~35%、3 人~75%、5 人~85%、3-5 人覆盖 74-87%)并不在这个 severity 页面,属于 Nielsen 关于启发式评审/评审员人数的另一篇文章;被误挂到本 URL。

URL 为旧版路径,302 重定向(developers→developer,且现行 canonical 为 /design/human-interface-guidelines/drag-and-drop,无 /macos/user-interaction/ 段)。页面主题是拖放(移动/复制内容),并不支持「每个可交互元素都必须响应 hover/click/right-click/drag、所有可交互元素必须有可见 hover 悬停态」这一广义结论——该 hover/指针内容属于 HIG 的 Pointing devices / Pointer interactions 页面,拖放页无法背书此声称。属于源-证不匹配。

dead_or_fabricated: 严重度页面第二条:发现率百分比(1 人~35%/3 人~75%/5 人~85%/3-5 人覆盖 74-87%)不在该 severity URL 上,系误挂——这些数字出自 Nielsen 另一篇关于启发式评审员人数的文章;本页只支持『3 名评审员均值、单人不可靠』部分。

Apple HIG 拖放 URL:①链接为已废弃的 developers.apple.com/.../macos/user-interaction/ 路径,发生 302 重定向,现行 canonical 是 /design/human-interface-guidelines/drag-and-drop;②该页只讲拖放操作,不能支撑『所有可交互元素必须有可见 hover 悬停态、必须响应 hover/click/right-click/drag』的广义主张(该主张应引 Pointing devices / Pointer interactions 页)。
